details.calculation: ($13,450,737 / 1,475 total beneficiaries) = $9,119.14 per outcome. | A $1,000 donation achieves ≈ 0.11 outcomes.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'Between 2003 and 2020, the stray animal population of Phuket was reduced by about 90%.'
details.formula: Highest level of evidence cited in impact claims

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

metrics.context_qualifier: Total animals neutered and vaccinated since 2003, preventing countless unwanted litters and reducing stray populations.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without intervention, these animals would have continued to reproduce, increasing stray populations and suffering.

metrics.evidence_quote: By 2024's end, our numbers are staggering; 1.36 million animals neutered, with over half in the past three years.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of stray dogs and cats neutered and vaccinated in 2024 through the CNVR program across Thailand.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without intervention, these animals would have continued to reproduce, increasing stray populations and suffering.

metrics.evidence_quote: In 2024 alone, we neutered a record 267,100 animals.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of sick and injured stray dogs and cats receiving medical care and rehabilitation in 2024.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without intervention, these animals would have continued to suffer from illness or injury, likely leading to death.

metrics.evidence_quote: This year, we treated 22,736 dogs and cats—an increase of 6%, delivering critical medical care, rehabilitation, and comfort to animals in need.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of treatments administered to 14,178 animals in the community through outreach programs in 2024.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without outreach, these animals would not have received necessary medical care.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of critical surgeries performed on sick and injured stray dogs and cats in 2024.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without these surgeries, the animals would likely have died or suffered severe, debilitating conditions.

metrics.evidence_quote: Behind the scenes, our veterinary surgeons performed 1,038 life-saving operations, while our front office staff managed 17,714 calls for help—a 4% rise over the previous year.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total number of dogs and cats adopted from the shelter to homes in Thailand and abroad in 2024.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without adoption, these animals would remain in the shelter or face an uncertain future on the streets.

metrics.evidence_quote: A total of 461 rescued animals – 308 dogs and 153 cats – left the Soi Dog shelter for loving homes in 2024.

metrics.context_qualifier: Amount of food provided to animals affected by severe flooding in northern and southern Thailand in 2024.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without intervention, these animals would have faced starvation due to natural disaster.

metrics.evidence_quote: 43.599 tonnes of food provided for animals affected by the floods

metrics.context_qualifier: Reduction in the stray animal population in Phuket between 2003 and 2020 due to CNVR efforts.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without CNVR, the stray population would likely have remained stable or increased.

metrics.evidence_quote: Between 2003 and 2020, the stray animal population of Phuket was reduced by about 90%.

metrics.context_qualifier: Only one confirmed case of rabies in Phuket since 1995 (in 2019) due to vaccination efforts.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without vaccination, rabies cases would likely be higher.

metrics.evidence_quote: As a result of the foundation's vaccination effort, Phuket has seen only one confirmed case of rabies since 1995, in 2019.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of children engaged through educational sessions and events in 2024 to foster animal welfare understanding.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without the program, these children would have less exposure to animal welfare education.

metrics.evidence_quote: The team also engaged 1,885 children through educational sessions and events, inspiring them to become compassionate advocates for animals.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total number of students reached by the humane education program from 2017 to 2020.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without the program, these students would not have received humane education.

metrics.evidence_quote: In 2017, Soi Dog launched a humane education programme, which totalled 15,058 students and 861 teachers by 2020.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of animal cruelty investigations launched by the social change team in 2024.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without intervention, these cruelty cases might go unaddressed.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of dogs successfully rehabilitated by the behaviour and enrichment team in 2024, preparing them for adoption.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without rehabilitation, these traumatized dogs would struggle to be adopted or thrive.

metrics.evidence_quote: Thanks to your generous support, they successfully rehabilitated 78 dogs, helping them to overcome past trauma, learn to trust, and develop the confidence needed to thrive in homes.

metrics.context_qualifier: Number of prosecutions initiated under the Animal Welfare Act in 2024 for animal cruelty cases.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without intervention, these cruelty cases might not lead to legal action.

metrics.evidence_quote: 4 prosecutions under the Animal Welfare Act

significant_events.event_name: Enactment of Thailand's First Animal Welfare Act
significant_events.source_quote: In 2014, the foundation's campaign led to the enactment of the Prevention of Animal Cruelty and Provision of Animal Welfare Act, the first animal welfare act in Thailand.
significant_events.summary: Soi Dog Foundation's campaign led to the enactment of the Prevention of Animal Cruelty and Provision of Animal Welfare Act in 2014, the first animal welfare act in Thailand.

significant_events.event_name: Ending Dog Meat Trade in Thailand and Regional Expansion
significant_events.source_quote: In 2024, we reaped the rewards of taking bold risks, embracing course corrections, and making fearless, swift decisions to find solutions for millions of animals suffering in this brutal and horrific trade. From becoming a legal entity in Vietnam to moving towards eradicating the remnants of the illegal trade in the Philippines, this incredible impact is only made possible because of your generosity.
significant_events.summary: Soi Dog successfully eliminated the dog meat trade in Thailand and expanded efforts to Cambodia, Philippines, and Vietnam, achieving systemic change and legislative amendments.

significant_events.event_name: Soi Dog Foundation Becomes Legal Entity in Vietnam
significant_events.source_quote: Our biggest milestone came in November, when Soi Dog Foundation officially became a legal entity in Vietnam after receiving an operating registration certificate, achieved in a record-breaking time frame of just seven months.
significant_events.summary: In November 2024, Soi Dog Foundation officially became a legal entity in Vietnam, receiving an operating registration certificate in seven months.

significant_events.event_name: Hanoi City-Wide Directive on Dog and Cat Meat Consumption
significant_events.source_quote: Our collaboration with the Vietnamese Government reached new heights in June. Hanoi authorities issued a city-wide directive focused on public health and rabies prevention, while also urging communities to move away from consuming dog and cat meat—a strong step towards aligning with modern global values.
significant_events.summary: In June 2024, Hanoi authorities issued a directive urging communities to move away from consuming dog and cat meat, aligning with public health and rabies prevention.

significant_events.event_name: Police Training and Arrests in Philippines Dog Meat Trade
significant_events.source_quote: In the Philippines, our police training efforts have led to numerous arrests.We also helped secure 10 arrests and 2 convictions of dog meat traders, sending a strong message: animal cruelty will not go unpunished.
significant_events.summary: Soi Dog's police training efforts in the Philippines led to numerous arrests, including 10 arrests and 2 convictions of dog meat traders in 2024.

significant_events.source_quote: This momentum carried through to November, when Bangkok became one of the first cities to join the Positive Cities Pledge—a global commitment to humane and sustainable stray dog population management.
significant_events.summary: In November 2024, Bangkok joined the Positive Cities Pledge, committing to humane and sustainable stray dog population management.

significant_events.source_quote: In September, we proudly launched our 19th mobile CNVR clinic, joining two others already stationed in Surat Thani, Thailand's sixth-largest province.
significant_events.summary: In September 2024, Soi Dog launched its 19th mobile CNVR clinic, expanding its capacity to improve the lives of street animals across Thailand.

significant_events.source_quote: In 2017, Soi Dog launched a humane education programme, which totalled 15,058 students and 861 teachers by 2020.
significant_events.summary: Soi Dog launched a humane education program in 2017, reaching over 15,000 students and 800 teachers by 2020.

significant_events.event_name: Emergency Aid for Animals in Ukraine
significant_events.source_quote: In 2022, animals outside of Asia were helped for the first time in Soi Dog's history when assistance was provided to refugees' pets and thousands of stray and abandoned animals affected by the war in Ukraine.
significant_events.summary: In 2022, Soi Dog provided assistance to refugees' pets and stray animals affected by the war in Ukraine, marking its first aid outside Asia.

data.original_text: Soi Dog Foundation is a nonprofit organisation dedicated to improving the welfare of stray dogs and cats across Asia. Its headquarters is in Phuket, Thailand, and it is a legally registered nonprofit organisation in Thailand, US, Canada, Australia, France, UK, Switzerland, and the Netherlands. Its primary goal is to care for homeless and abused dogs in Thailand.
Its name, "Soi Dog", is derived from the Thai word soi, meaning "street", reflecting its mission to care for the street dogs and cats of Thailand.

Soi Dog's efforts include spaying and neutering to prevent unwanted litters, and providing rescue, vaccination, medical treatment, shelter, and adoption services for animals unable to return to the streets.
The foundation has saved thousands of dogs from illegal trades, particularly for meat, and works to reduce free-ranging dog and cat populations through sterilisation.
Every year, Soi Dog spays/neuters and vaccinates tens of thousands of stray dogs and cats throughout Thailand, and in 2023, it surpassed the milestone of one million animals neutered since 2003.
In 2014, the foundation's campaign led to the enactment of the Prevention of Animal Cruelty and Provision of Animal Welfare Act, the first animal welfare act in Thailand.
Between 2003 and 2020, the stray animal population of Phuket was reduced by about 90%.
As a result of the foundation's vaccination effort, Phuket has seen only one confirmed case of rabies since 1995, in 2019.
In 2023, Soi Dog surpassed the milestone of one million animals neutered and vaccinated since 2003.
As of April 2024, they had neutered and vaccinated 1,177,730 dogs and cats, making their CNVR programme the largest of its kind in the world.

Soi Dog successfully eliminated the dog meat trade in Thailand and has also expanded its efforts to neighbouring countries, including Cambodia, the Philippines, Vietnam, and other Southeast Asian countries where this issue is widespread.
In 2017, Soi Dog launched a humane education programme, which totalled 15,058 students and 861 teachers by 2020.

In 2023, the foundation's short film Love Will Always Triumph Over Evil, which depicted the story of Cola, a puppy whose front legs were amputated with a sword by an angry man, received the British Charity Film Award in London.
In 2020, John Dalley was included in the Queen's Birthday Honours List and was awarded the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire for his work with the foundation.
In 2023, Soi Dog was named Best Animal Welfare in Asia Non-Profit Organisation by Acquisition International.
For over 20 years, we've created lasting change for millions of dogs and cats in Asia. We rescue, neuter, work with communities, and advocate for animal welfare.
We help stray animals affected by man-made or natural disasters across the region and are regularly called upon to provide food, rescue and emergency aid to animals affected by monsoon rains.
Our dedicated team of rescue officers help thousands of animals in need each year, working seven days a week to ensure every report of an animal in need is responded to as swiftly as possible.
We provide high quality treatment for hundreds of sick and injured stray dogs and cats in our shelter hospitals, while our outreach programme cares for stray animals in the heart of the community.
Our world-leading CNVR programme is reducing the number of animals born into a life of suffering on the streets and helping to eliminate rabies, benefitting animal and human communities alike.
Instrumental in ending the horrendous dog meat trade in Thailand, we continue to work to end the trade in other Southeast Asian countries where it is prevalent.

Spread across 12 acres, the Gill Dalley Sanctuary in Phuket is home to over 1,800 dogs and cats. Here, they receive the physical and emotional rehabilitation they need to help prepare them for adoption.
We adopt both locally and internationally – finding loving homes for deserving dogs and cats rescued from dire conditions on the streets.

To improve the welfare of dogs and cats in Asia, resulting in better lives for both the animal and human communities, to create a society without homeless animals, and to ultimately end animal cruelty.
Every year, Soi Dog spays/neuters and vaccinates tens of thousands of street dogs and cats in Phuket, Bangkok, and other provinces of Thailand, and has to date sterilised more than 199,000 animals.

Soi Dog Foundation USA supports and promotes Soi Dog Foundation's ongoing animal welfare efforts in Thailand and beyond which include the world's largest spay/neuter initiative (over 1 million stray animals neutered and vaccinated since 2003), community outreach and humane education programs, and fighting the dog and cat meat trade throughout Asia.

Results: Over 1 million dogs and cats sterilized, elimination of the dog meat trade in Thailand, building the first hospital in Asia dedicated to the treatment of stray and abandoned animals and meat trade rescues, thousands of rescued dogs adopted into loving homes.
Target demographics: Support and promote the efforts of Soi Dog Foundation in Southeast Asia.

Direct beneficiaries per year: Neuter and vaccinate almost 200,000 dogs and cats, provide veterinary treatment to over 17,000 animals, deliver hundreds of tons of food and urgent supplies to tens of thousands of stricken animals affected by natural and man-made disasters, save dogs and cats from the meat trade in the Philippines.
Geographic areas served: Thailand, Vietnam, Philippines.

Soi Dog Foundation actively gathers information and evidence in animal cruelty cases to aid prosecutions, and works to apply gentle pressure in an attempt to get the courts to impose the maximum penalties on those who are cruel to animals.

Soi Dog oversees local, national and international adoptions, directly rehoming thousands of dogs and cats rescued from dire situations across the region, from government shelters, and from the horrendous dog and cat meat trade.
Last year, we matched over 450 dogs and cats with their forever families.

For animals unable to be returned safely to the streets, Soi Dog provides sanctuary - a haven where sick, injured and vulnerable dogs and cats receive world-class medical care, and love. Spread across 12 acres, the Gill Dalley Sanctuary is home to over 1,800 dogs and cats.

As part of a wider community engagement initiative, Soi Dog runs humane education, community liaison and outreach programmes targeted to help improve the welfare of both stray and domestic animals, educating local schoolchildren and the wider public on animal welfare whilst providing support to network of volunteer 'feeders', caring members of the local community who act as guardians for stray animals.
Volunteers walk and socialise with dogs and cats, helping them trust people and learn how to walk on a lead.
Today, the team sterilizes over 20,000 animals every month, helping prevent more suffering in the long run.
In 2019, Soi Dog Foundation achieved over 100,000 spay/neuter procedures and is now neutering and vaccinating more stray animals than any other organisation in the world.
In 2024 alone, we neutered a record 267,100 animals.
After two decades of relentless effort, Soi Dog now operates the world's largest CNVR (Catch, Neuter, Vaccinate and Return) programme.
By 2024's end, our numbers are staggering; 1.36 million animals neutered, with over half in the past three years.

Beyond healthier streets and fewer sick dogs, we have seen a shift in community attitudes and official support. In Bangkok, the authorities back our work by providing clinic locations and promoting responsible pet ownership.

This momentum carried through to November, when Bangkok became one of the first cities to join the Positive Cities Pledge—a global commitment to humane and sustainable stray dog population management. Through a growing partnership with the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration (BMA), dog population management has become a shared priority.
A total of 461 rescued animals – 308 dogs and 153 cats – left the Soi Dog shelter for loving homes in 2024.

In 2022, animals outside of Asia were helped for the first time in Soi Dog's history when assistance was provided to refugees' pets and thousands of stray and abandoned animals affected by the war in Ukraine.
